title: Correlation-Driven Spin Reorientation via Competing Anisotropy Channels in CrPS4
authors: Raju Baral, Harald O. Jeschke, Igor I. Mazin, Jue Liu, David Mandrus, Stuart Calder
abstract: We identify a correlation-driven mechanism for the temperature-induced spin reorientation in the quasi-one-dimensional van der Waals antiferromagnet CrPS4. Magnetic pair distribution function (mPDF) analysis resolves the local spin direction and shows that ferromagnetic intrachain correlations persist far above TN. Combining these correlations with a DFT-derived spin Hamiltonian reveals competing single-ion and exchange-anisotropy channels, with single-ion anisotropy remaining local while exchange anisotropy is renormalized as intersite correlations decay. This differential renormalization rotates the effective easy axis and captures the ordered-state canting. Above TN, the continued rotation beyond the model prediction delineates the limits of the dominant-chain approximation. These results establish mPDF-derived correlations as direct inputs to microscopic Hamiltonians and show how low-dimensional correlations can control magnetic anisotropy.
summary: This paper demonstrates that the spin reorientation in CrPS4 is caused by the temperature-dependent decay of magnetic correlations, which affects exchange anisotropy differently than single-ion anisotropy. By combining neutron scattering with ab initio calculations, the authors provide a quantitative microscopic description of how low-dimensional correlations control magnetic anisotropy.
main_problem: Identifying the mechanism behind the temperature-induced spin reorientation transition (SRT) in the quasi-1D van der Waals antiferromagnet CrPS4.
main_result: The SRT is driven by a differential renormalization where decaying intersite correlations suppress exchange-anisotropy channels while single-ion anisotropy remains local, causing the effective easy axis to rotate.
method: The study combines magnetic pair distribution function (mPDF) analysis of neutron total scattering with DFT+U calculations for energy mapping and classical Monte Carlo simulations.
model_or_system: The system is CrPS4, a quasi-1D van der Waals antiferromagnetic semiconductor with A-type magnetic order. The model uses a microscopic spin Hamiltonian including isotropic exchange, exchange-anisotropy, and single-ion anisotropy terms.
key_observables: Magnetic pair distribution function (mPDF), local magnetic order parameter (m), spin polar angle (theta), correlation length (xi), and Cr-Cr bond distances (d1, d2).
important_parameters: Neeel temperature (TN = 37 K), Hubbard U (2 eV), exchange couplings (J1, J2), and spin-spin correlation functions.
main_assumptions: The model uses a dominant-chain approximation focusing on n=1 and n=2 bonds and neglects Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya interactions due to inversion symmetry.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows a schematic of the system; Figure 3 compares the correlation-renormalization factor and predicted spin angles against experimental data, and shows structural anomalies in Cr-Cr bond distances at TN.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the spin reorientation problem, describes the mPDF and DFT+U methodology, presents the results of local correlation measurements, develops the anisotropy renormalization model, and discusses the connection to structural anomalies.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Magnetic structure of CrPS4, where the Cr spins are canted by 12◦from the c axis at 2 K. The six nearest-neighbor Cr–Cr shells (n1–n6) used in the correlation analysis are la- beled, with n1,n2 forming the dominant intrachain bonds.

FIG. 2. Temperature evolution of the (a) local magnetic order parameter, (b) spin angle, (c) correlation length for T > TN, and (d) shell-resolved spin-spin correlation function, ⟨S0 · Sn⟩, of the first six nearest neighbors for T > TN. Positive values correspond to net ferromagnetic (FM) and negative to antiferromagnetic (AFM) correlations. The persistence of positive n = 1, 2 correlations to 100 K demonstrates that the paramagnetic state remains locally chain-like even after the collapse of long-range correlations.

FIG. 3. (a) Correlation-renormalization factor ξ(T) from the moment-based estimate ξ = 2m2/(1 + m4) (blue circles, filled for T ≤TN, open above TN) and the directly measured intrachain correlation ξexp = ⟨S0 ·S1⟩from mPDF analysis (used as model input for T > TN). The mPDF correlation length ξmPDF is shown on the right axis. Agreement near TN supports the use of the Gaussian estimator in the ordered phase. (b) Model-predicted equilibrium spin angle θmin(T) (triangles) compared with the experimentally refined spin angle θexp(T). Below TN, θmin is computed using the moment-based ξ and above TN it is computed using the directly measured ξexp. The dashed horizontal line marks the DFT+U...
